---
title: "Christopher Hu"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: FALSE
---

<img src="headshot.jpeg" id="headshot" style="border:4px solid #D3D3D3" width="270" align="left" hspace="25" vspace="70/"/>

**About Me:**

I am a PhD candidate in the social foundations of education program at the University of Virginia's School of Education and Human Development. My research primarily draws from anthropological and sociological perspectives in order to understand and examine race, ethnicity, and achievement in educational contexts. I am currently working on my dissertation---an ethnography of Chinese and Taiwanese upper-middle-class families in the New Jersey ethnoburb. I am interested in the ways that class and ethnicity intersect as forms of social organization and the ways that immigrant communities construct collective educational practices.

My versatile and emerging research skills include (but not limited to) ethnography, participant observation, interviewing, survey design and analysis, qualitative coding, thematic analysis, discourse analysis, geospatial analysis, data visualization, and quantitative analysis with proficiency in R, Excel, SPSS, & Stata.

If you would like to learn more about me, you can find [my CV](CV_profile.html) and some of my sample projects.

**Education:**

**Ph.D.** Education - Social Foundations, University of Virginia, 2025 (anticipated)

**M.Ed.** Educational Psychology - Social Foundations, University of Virginia, 2020

**B.S.** Biochemistry, Washington and Lee University, 2015

**Contact:** [ch7dm\@virginia.edu](mailto:ch7dm@virginia.edu)
